title: Enhancer hubs govern chromatin topology and Th17 identity
authors: Siklenka K, Zhang C, Li L, Parker ME, Mehta NU, Barrera A, Venukuttan R, Crawford GE, Gersbach CA, Ciofani M, Reddy TE
abstract: A wealth of noncoding regulatory elements has been described across mammalian cell types, yet determining their functional role remains a challenge. Regulatory control of gene expression is critical during cell differentiation and identity maintenance. This study examines enhancer hubs that govern chromatin topology and Th17 cell identity.
note: Methods-relevant only. Enhancer hub and chromatin topology approaches applicable to autism regulatory analysis, but study content is immunology (Th17 cells), not brain or autism.
